exampleSentencesTranslated: Watch out for cars when you cross the street!
You have to watch out for your wallet in crowded places.
Watch out, it might rain heavily when you finish work.
exampleSentences: Coi chừng xe khi em băng qua đường nhé!
Anh phải coi chừng ví của mình ở chỗ đông người.
Chị ơi, coi chừng trời mưa to lúc tan làm nha.
notes:
Coi chừng = “watch out” or “be careful.” You use it to warn someone about danger or a possible bad situation.

It’s a verb phrase. Put it before the thing you’re warning about (a person, object, or situation).

It can also mean “be careful or something bad might happen,” with a slightly serious tone. In the North, people more often say cẩn thận; in the South, coi chừng is very common.
